title: [28～31]다음을 읽고 (   )에 들어갈 내용으로 가장 알맞은 것을 고르십시오. (각 2점)

content.q_text: 새를 상자 안에 넣으면 자꾸 밖으로 나오려고 한다. 그러나 반대로 자유로운 공간에서는 안정을 찾고 도망치려고 애쓰지 않는다. 아이도 이와 마찬가지다. 부모들이 (   ) 하면 아이는 이를 구속이라 생각하고 반발심을 가진다. 그러나 일어나는 시간, 방과 후 할 일 등 생활 규칙을 아이와 의논하여 정하게 되면 부모가 시키지 않아도 스스로 책임감을 가지고 잘 지키려고 노력한다.

explain.vi:
Khi bạn đặt một con chim vào hộp, nó sẽ tiếp tục cố gắng chui ra. Ngược lại, nếu được ở trong không gian tự do, nó sẽ thấy ổn định và không tìm cách trốn chạy. Đối với trẻ con cũng vậy. Nếu cha mẹ cứ ( ), đứa trẻ sẽ nghĩ rằng đó là sự gò ép và có thái độ oán giận. Tuy nhiên, nếu các quy tắc trong cuộc sống, chẳng hạn như thời gian thức dậy hay các việc phải làm sau giờ học, được quyết định với sự tham khảo ý kiến ​​của con trẻ, chúng sẽ tự chịu trách nhiệm và cố gắng thực hiện tốt, ngay cả khi cha mẹ không yêu cầu chúng làm vậy. 

1. Muốn bắt học thêm sau giờ học
2. Muốn tham gia vào mối quan hệ với bạn bè
3. Ra quy định và bắt làm theo như vậy
4. Không cho nuôi động vật ở nhà
--------------------
그대로 따르다: Làm theo như vậy
애쓰지 않다: Không cố gắng để làm việc gì đó.
이와 마찬가지다: Giống như vậy.

Câu sau đề cập đến việc cùng con thảo luận và cùng tạo ra các quy định nên câu trước điền đáp án 3 sẽ có sự liên kết.
explain.en:
1. Force to study after school
2. Involve in children's relationships with friends
3. Set rules and let them follow them
4. Do not allow pets in the house.
--------------------
When you put a bird in a box, it keeps trying to come out. However, in a free space, on the other hand, it finds stability and does not try to escape. So are the children. When parents set rules and let the children follow them, the children think of them as restraints and have a resentment. However, if the rules of life, such as wake up time and after-school tasks, are discussed with the children, they will take responsibility for themselves and try to keep it well, even if the parents do not ask them to do so.

<g>그대로 따르다:</g> To follow as it is
<g>애쓰지 않다:</g> Do not try to do something
<g>이와 마찬가지다:</g> Like that

The following sentence refers to discussing and creating rules with the children, so the previous sentence with 3 will have a connection.
explain.fr:
1. Forcer à étudier après l'école
2. Impliquez-vous dans les relations des enfants avec leurs amis
3. Établissez des règles et laissez-les les suivre
4. Ne laissez pas les animaux domestiques dans la maison.
--------------------
Lorsque vous mettez un oiseau dans une boîte, il continue d'essayer d'en sortir. Cependant, dans un espace libre, en revanche, il trouve la stabilité et ne cherche pas à s'échapper. Ainsi sont les enfants. Lorsque les parents établissent des règles et laissent les enfants les suivre, les enfants les considèrent comme des contraintes et ont du ressentiment. Cependant, si les règles de vie, telles que l'heure du réveil et les tâches après l'école, sont discutées avec les enfants, ils se responsabiliseront et essaieront de bien le faire, même si les parents ne le leur demandent pas.

<g>그대로 따르다:</g>A suivre tel quel
<g>애쓰지 않다:</g>N'essayez pas de faire quelque chose
<g>이와 마찬가지다:</g>Comme ça

La phrase suivante fait référence à la discussion et à la création de règles avec les enfants, de sorte que la phrase précédente avec 3 aura un lien.

#######

1. Force to study after school
2. Involve in children's relationships with friends
3. Set rules and let them follow them
4. Do not allow pets in the house.
--------------------
When you put a bird in a box, it keeps trying to come out. However, in a free space, on the other hand, it finds stability and does not try to escape. So are the children. When parents set rules and let the children follow them, the children think of them as restraints and have a resentment. However, if the rules of life, such as wake up time and after-school tasks, are discussed with the children, they will take responsibility for themselves and try to keep it well, even if the parents do not ask them to do so.

<g>그대로 따르다:</g> To follow as it is
<g>애쓰지 않다:</g> Do not try to do something
<g>이와 마찬가지다:</g> Like that

The following sentence refers to discussing and creating rules with the children, so the previous sentence with 3 will have a connection.
explain.es:
1. Obligar a estudiar después de la escuela
2. Involucrarse en las relaciones de los niños con sus amigos
3. Establece reglas y deja que las sigan
4. No permita mascotas en la casa.
--------------------
Cuando pones un pájaro en una caja, sigue intentando salir. Sin embargo, en un espacio libre, en cambio, encuentra estabilidad y no intenta escapar. Así son los niños. Cuando los padres establecen reglas y dejan que los niños las sigan, los niños las consideran restricciones y tienen resentimiento. Sin embargo, si las reglas de la vida, como la hora de levantarse y las tareas después de la escuela, se discuten con los niños, se responsabilizarán por sí mismos y tratarán de cumplirla bien, incluso si los padres no se lo piden.

<g>그대로 따르다:</g>Para seguir como es
<g>애쓰지 않다:</g>no trates de hacer algo
<g>이와 마찬가지다:</g>Como eso

La siguiente oración se refiere a discutir y crear reglas con los niños, por lo que la oración anterior con 3 tendrá una conexión.

#######

1. Force to study after school
2. Involve in children's relationships with friends
3. Set rules and let them follow them
4. Do not allow pets in the house.
--------------------
When you put a bird in a box, it keeps trying to come out. However, in a free space, on the other hand, it finds stability and does not try to escape. So are the children. When parents set rules and let the children follow them, the children think of them as restraints and have a resentment. However, if the rules of life, such as wake up time and after-school tasks, are discussed with the children, they will take responsibility for themselves and try to keep it well, even if the parents do not ask them to do so.

<g>애쓰지 않다:</g> Do not try to do something
<g>이와 마찬가지다:</g> Like that

The following sentence refers to discussing and creating rules with the children, so the previous sentence with 3 will have a connection.
explain.id:
1. Paksa belajar sepulang sekolah
2. Terlibat dalam hubungan anak dengan teman
3. Tetapkan aturan dan biarkan mereka mengikutinya
4. Jangan biarkan hewan peliharaan masuk ke dalam rumah.
--------------------
Ketika Anda memasukkan seekor burung ke dalam sebuah kotak, ia terus berusaha untuk keluar. Namun, di ruang bebas, di sisi lain, ia menemukan stabilitas dan tidak mencoba melarikan diri. Begitu juga anak-anak. Ketika orang tua menetapkan aturan dan membiarkan anak-anak mengikutinya, anak-anak menganggapnya sebagai pengekangan dan memiliki kebencian. Namun, jika aturan hidup, seperti waktu bangun dan tugas sepulang sekolah, didiskusikan dengan anak-anak, mereka akan bertanggung jawab atas diri mereka sendiri dan berusaha untuk menjaganya dengan baik, bahkan jika orang tua tidak meminta mereka untuk melakukannya.

<g>그대로 따르다:</g>Untuk mengikuti apa adanya
<g>애쓰지 않다:</g>Jangan mencoba melakukan sesuatu
<g>이와 마찬가지다:</g>Seperti itu

Kalimat berikut mengacu pada membahas dan membuat aturan dengan anak-anak, sehingga kalimat sebelumnya dengan 3 akan memiliki koneksi.

2. Involve in children's relationships with friends
3. Set rules and let them follow them
4. Do not allow pets in the house.

The following sentence refers to discussing and creating rules with the children, so the previous sentence with 3 will have a connection.
explain.ja:
1. 放課後の強制勉強
2. 子供の友達との関係に参加する
3. ルールを設定し、従わせる
4. 家にペットを入れないでください。
--------------------
鳥を箱に入れると、鳥は出ようとし続けます。しかし一方で自由空間では、安定を見つけて逃げようとはしません。子供たちもそうです。親がルールを作って子供に守らせると、子供はそれを束縛だと思い、恨みを持ちます。しかし、起床時間や放課後の宿題など生活のルールを子供たちと話し合っておけば、親に言われなくても自分で責任を持って守ろうとします。

<g>그대로 따르다:</g>ありのままに従うこと
<g>애쓰지 않다:</g>何かをしようとしないでください
<g>이와 마찬가지다:</g>そのように

次の文は、子供たちと話し合ってルールを作成することを指しているため、前の 3 の文と関連があります。

#######

1. Force to study after school
2. Involve in children's relationships with friends
3. Set rules and let them follow them
4. Do not allow pets in the house.
--------------------
When you put a bird in a box, it keeps trying to come out. However, in a free space, on the other hand, it finds stability and does not try to escape. So are the children. When parents set rules and let the children follow them, the children think of them as restraints and have a resentment. However, if the rules of life, such as wake up time and after-school tasks, are discussed with the children, they will take responsibility for themselves and try to keep it well, even if the parents do not ask them to do so.

<g>애쓰지 않다:</g> Do not try to do something

The following sentence refers to discussing and creating rules with the children, so the previous sentence with 3 will have a connection.
explain.de:
1. Nach der Schule zum Lernen zwingen
2. Beteiligen Sie sich an den Beziehungen der Kinder zu Freunden
3. Legen Sie Regeln fest und lassen Sie sie sie befolgen
4. Lassen Sie keine Haustiere im Haus zu.
--------------------
Wenn Sie einen Vogel in eine Kiste stecken, versucht er immer wieder herauszukommen. In einem freien Raum hingegen findet es Halt und versucht nicht zu entkommen. Die Kinder sind es auch. Wenn Eltern Regeln aufstellen und die Kinder ihnen folgen lassen, betrachten die Kinder sie als Beschränkungen und haben einen Groll. Wenn jedoch die Lebensregeln wie Weckzeit und Aufgaben nach der Schule mit den Kindern besprochen werden, übernehmen sie die Verantwortung für sich selbst und versuchen, diese gut einzuhalten, auch wenn die Eltern sie nicht dazu auffordern.

<g>그대로 따르다:</g>Folgen wie es ist
<g>애쓰지 않다:</g>Versuchen Sie nicht, etwas zu tun
<g>이와 마찬가지다:</g>So wie das

Der folgende Satz bezieht sich auf das Besprechen und Erstellen von Regeln mit den Kindern, daher wird der vorherige Satz mit 3 einen Zusammenhang haben.

#######

1. Force to study after school
2. Involve in children's relationships with friends
3. Set rules and let them follow them
4. Do not allow pets in the house.
--------------------
When you put a bird in a box, it keeps trying to come out. However, in a free space, on the other hand, it finds stability and does not try to escape. So are the children. When parents set rules and let the children follow them, the children think of them as restraints and have a resentment. <g>애쓰지 않다:</g> Do not try to do something

The following sentence refers to discussing and creating rules with the children, so the previous sentence with 3 will have a connection.
explain.zh-CN:
1.放学后强制学习
2. 参与孩子与朋友的关系
3. 制定规则并让他们遵守
4. 不要让宠物进入屋内。
--------------------
当你把一只鸟放在一个盒子里时，它会一直试图出来。然而，另一方面，在自由空间中，它找到了稳定性，不会试图逃跑。孩子们也是如此。当父母制定规则，让孩子遵守时，孩子会认为它们是约束，并产生怨恨。但是，如果与孩子讨论起床时间和课后任务等生活规则，即使父母不要求他们这样做，他们也会对自己负责并尽量保持良好。

<g>그대로 따르다:</g>照原样跟随
<g>애쓰지 않다:</g>不要试图做某事
<g>이와 마찬가지다:</g>像那样

下面这句话是指和孩子一起讨论和创造规则，所以上一句与3会有联系。

2. Involve in children's relationships with friends
3. Set rules and let them follow them
4. Do not allow pets in the house.
--------------------
When you put a bird in a box, it keeps trying to come out. However, in a free space, on the other hand, it finds stability and does not try to escape. So are the children. When parents set rules and let the children follow them, the children think of them as restraints and have a resentment. The following sentence refers to discussing and creating rules with the children, so the previous sentence with 3 will have a connection.
explain.zh-TW:
1.放學後強制學習
2. 參與孩子與朋友的關係
3. 制定規則並讓他們遵守
4. 不要讓寵物進入屋內。
--------------------
當你把一隻鳥放在一個盒子裡時，它會一直試圖出來。然而，另一方面，在自由空間中，它找到了穩定性，不會試圖逃跑。孩子們也是如此。當父母制定規則，讓孩子遵守時，孩子會認為它們是約束，並產生怨恨。但是，如果與孩子討論起床時間和課後任務等生活規則，即使父母不要求他們這樣做，他們也會對自己負責並儘量保持良好。

<g>그대로 따르다:</g>照原樣跟隨
<g>애쓰지 않다:</g>不要試圖做某事
<g>이와 마찬가지다:</g>像那樣

下面這句話是指和孩子一起討論和創造規則，所以上一句與3會有聯繫。

When you put a bird in a box, it keeps trying to come out. However, in a free space, on the other hand, it finds stability and does not try to escape. So are the children. The following sentence refers to discussing and creating rules with the children, so the previous sentence with 3 will have a connection.
explain.it:
1. Forza a studiare dopo la scuola
2. Coinvolgi i bambini nelle relazioni con gli amici
3. Stabilisci delle regole e lascia che le seguano
4. Non ammettere animali in casa.
--------------------
Quando metti un uccellino in una scatola, continua a cercare di uscire. Tuttavia, in uno spazio libero, invece, trova stabilità e non cerca di scappare. Così sono i bambini. Quando i genitori stabiliscono delle regole e lasciano che i figli le seguano, i bambini le considerano come restrizioni e provano risentimento. Tuttavia, se si discutono con i bambini le regole della vita, come l'ora della sveglia e i compiti del doposcuola, questi si assumeranno la responsabilità e cercheranno di mantenerla bene, anche se i genitori non glielo chiedono.

<g>그대로 따르다:</g>Da seguire così com'è
<g>애쓰지 않다:</g>Non cercare di fare qualcosa
<g>이와 마찬가지다:</g>Come quello

La frase seguente si riferisce alla discussione e alla creazione di regole con i bambini, quindi la frase precedente con 3 avrà una connessione.

#######

1. Force to study after school
2. Involve in children's relationships with friends
3. Set rules and let them follow them
4. Do not allow pets in the house.
--------------------
When you put a bird in a box, it keeps trying to come out. However, in a free space, on the other hand, it finds stability and does not try to escape. So are the children. When parents set rules and let the children follow them, the children think of them as restraints and have a resentment. However, if the rules of life, such as wake up time and after-school tasks, are discussed with the children, they will take responsibility for themselves and try to keep it well, even if the parents do not ask them to do so.

The following sentence refers to discussing and creating rules with the children, so the previous sentence with 3 will have a connection.
explain.ru:
1. Заставить учиться после школы
2. Вовлекайте детей в общение с друзьями.
3. Установите правила и позвольте им следовать
4. Не пускайте в дом домашних животных.
--------------------
Когда вы кладете птицу в коробку, она все время пытается выбраться. Однако в свободном пространстве он обретает устойчивость и не пытается убежать. Как и дети. Когда родители устанавливают правила и позволяют детям следовать им, дети воспринимают их как ограничения и возмущаются. Однако если с детьми обсуждаются правила жизни, такие как время пробуждения и послешкольные задания, они возьмут ответственность на себя и постараются сохранить ее в порядке, даже если родители не просят их об этом.

<g>그대로 따르다:</g>Следовать как есть
<g>애쓰지 않다:</g>Не пытайтесь что-то сделать
<g>이와 마찬가지다:</g>Как это

Следующее предложение относится к обсуждению и созданию правил с детьми, поэтому предыдущее предложение с 3 будет иметь связь.

The following sentence refers to discussing and creating rules with the children, so the previous sentence with 3 will have a connection.
explain.th:
1.บังคับเรียนหลังเลิกเรียน
2. มีส่วนร่วมในความสัมพันธ์ระหว่างเด็กกับเพื่อนๆ
3. ตั้งกฎเกณฑ์แล้วปล่อยให้เป็นไปตามนั้น
4.ห้ามนำสัตว์เลี้ยงเข้ามาในบ้าน
--------------------
เมื่อคุณใส่นกลงในกล่อง นกจะพยายามออกมาเรื่อยๆ อย่างไรก็ตาม ในทางกลับกัน ในพื้นที่ว่าง จะพบความมั่นคงและไม่พยายามหลบหนี เด็กๆก็เช่นกัน เมื่อพ่อแม่ตั้งกฎเกณฑ์และปล่อยให้ลูกปฏิบัติตาม เด็กๆ จะมองว่ากฎเกณฑ์ดังกล่าวเป็นเครื่องพันธนาการและมีความขุ่นเคือง อย่างไรก็ตาม หากมีการหารือเกี่ยวกับกฎเกณฑ์ของชีวิต เช่น เวลาตื่นนอนและงานหลังเลิกเรียนกับเด็ก ๆ พวกเขาจะรับผิดชอบตนเองและพยายามรักษาไว้อย่างดีแม้ว่าผู้ปกครองจะไม่ขอให้ทำเช่นนั้นก็ตาม

<g>그대로 따르다:</g>ให้เป็นไปตามที่เป็นอยู่
<g>애쓰지 않다:</g>อย่าพยายามทำอะไรสักอย่าง

explain.pt:
1. Forçar a estudar depois da escola
2. Envolva-se nas relações das crianças com os amigos
3. Estabeleça regras e deixe-os segui-las
4. Não permita animais de estimação em casa.
--------------------
Quando você coloca um pássaro em uma caixa, ele fica tentando sair. Porém, em um espaço livre, por outro lado, encontra estabilidade e não tenta escapar. As crianças também. Quando os pais estabelecem regras e deixam os filhos segui-las, os filhos pensam nelas como restrições e ficam ressentidos. Porém, se as regras de vida, como a hora de acordar e as tarefas extracurriculares, forem discutidas com os filhos, eles assumirão a responsabilidade por si próprios e tentarão cumpri-la bem, mesmo que os pais não lhes peçam para o fazer.

<g>그대로 따르다:</g>Para seguir como está
<g>애쓰지 않다:</g>Não tente fazer algo
<g>이와 마찬가지다:</g>Assim

explain.hi:
1. स्कूल के बाद पढ़ाई के लिए मजबूर करना
2. दोस्तों के साथ बच्चों के संबंधों में शामिल हों
3. नियम निर्धारित करें और उन्हें उनका पालन करने दें
4. पालतू जानवरों को घर में न आने दें।
------------------------
जब आप किसी पक्षी को डिब्बे में बंद करते हैं तो वह बाहर आने की कोशिश करता रहता है। हालाँकि, दूसरी ओर, मुक्त स्थान में, यह स्थिरता पाता है और भागने की कोशिश नहीं करता है। वैसे ही बच्चे भी हैं. जब माता-पिता नियम निर्धारित करते हैं और बच्चों को उनका पालन करने देते हैं, तो बच्चे उन्हें प्रतिबंध मानते हैं और उनमें नाराजगी होती है। हालाँकि, अगर जीवन के नियमों, जैसे जागने का समय और स्कूल के बाद के कार्यों पर बच्चों के साथ चर्चा की जाए, तो वे अपनी ज़िम्मेदारी लेंगे और इसे अच्छी तरह से रखने की कोशिश करेंगे, भले ही माता-पिता उन्हें ऐसा करने के लिए न कहें।

<g>그대로 따르다:</g>जैसा है वैसा ही पालन करना
<g>애쓰지 않다:</g>कुछ करने की कोशिश मत करो
<g>이와 마찬가지다:</g>उस तरह

निम्नलिखित वाक्य बच्चों के साथ चर्चा करने और नियम बनाने को संदर्भित करता है, इसलिए 3 वाले पिछले वाक्य में एक संबंध होगा।

content.q_text: 사람들은 문화생활을 위한 시간을 따로 내기가 어렵다고들 한다. 최근 이런 사람들을 위해 (   ) 새로운 문화 콘텐츠들이 등장하고 있다. 그중 하나가 웹 소설인데 이것은 5분 이내에 읽을 수 있는 짧은 분량의 인터넷 소설을 말한다. 이 소설은 컴퓨터 화면에서 읽어도 눈이 피로하지 않도록 줄 간격이 넓게 편집되어 있다. 그리고 소설의 내용을 쉽게 이해할 수 있도록 등장인물의 수를 제한하는 것도 특징이다.
q_answer: 짧은 시간 동안 부담 없이 즐길 수 있는

explain.vi:
Người ta nói rằng rất khó để dành thời gian cho đời sống văn hóa. Gần đây, ( ) những người như vậy, content (nội dung) mới dần xuất hiện. Một trong số đó là tiểu thuyết web - những tiểu thuyết ngắn trên Internet có thể đọc xong trong vòng 5 phút. Loại tiểu thuyết này được biên tập với khoảng cách dòng rộng để mắt không bị mỏi ngay cả khi đọc trên màn hình máy tính. Nó cũng có đặc điểm là giới hạn số lượng nhân vật để (người đọc) có thể dễ dàng hiểu được nội dung của tiểu thuyết.

1. Thu hút sự chú ý với nội dung thú vị
2. Nhân vật xuất hiện trong tiểu thuyết là người hiện đại
3. Được tạo ra bằng cách biên kịch lại những cuốn tiểu thuyết thịnh hành
4. Người đọc có thể tận hưởng trong thời gian ngắn mà không bị áp lực gì cả
--------------------
N + 을 위한: Vì, để.
A/V + (으)ㄴ/는/(으)ㄹ + N: Định ngữ.
V + 도록: Để cho.

Câu đầu nói đến việc không có thời gian để dành cho đời sống văn hóa nên câu sau cần điền đáp án 4 để có sự liên kết.
explain.en:
1. Get attention with interesting content
2. Characters appearing in the novel are modern people
3. Created by rewriting popular novels
4. Readers can enjoy in a short time without any pressure
--------------------
People say that it is difficult to set aside time for cultural life. Recently, new cultural contents that can be enjoyed casually for a short period of time are emerging for such people. One of them is web novels, which are short Internet novels that can be read in less than 5 minutes. This novel is edited with wide line spacing so that the eyes do not get tired even when reading on a computer screen. It is also characterized by limiting the number of characters so that the content of the novel can be easily understood.

<g>N + 을/를 위해(서), V + 기 위해(서)</g>: For (the sake of), (in order) to.
<g>A/V + (으)ㄴ/는/(으)ㄹ + N:</g> Noun modifier.
<g>V + 도록:</g> In order to, so that.

The first sentence mentions that there is no time to spend in cultural life, so the second sentence needs to be fill in with 4 to have a connection.
explain.fr:
1. Attirez l'attention avec un contenu intéressant
2. Les personnages apparaissant dans le roman sont des gens modernes
3. Créé en réécrivant des romans populaires
4. Les lecteurs peuvent profiter en peu de temps sans aucune pression
--------------------
On dit qu'il est difficile de réserver du temps à la vie culturelle. Récemment, de nouveaux contenus culturels qui peuvent être appréciés avec désinvolture pendant une courte période de temps font leur apparition pour ces personnes. L'un d'eux est les romans Web, qui sont de courts romans Internet qui peuvent être lus en moins de 5 minutes. Ce roman est édité avec un interligne large afin que les yeux ne se fatiguent pas même lors de la lecture sur un écran d'ordinateur. Il se caractérise également par la limitation du nombre de caractères afin que le contenu du roman puisse être facilement compris.

<g>N + 을/를 위해(서), V + 기 위해(서)</g>: Pour (pour), (pour) faire.
<g>A/V + (으)ㄴ/는/(으)ㄹ + N:</g>Modificateur de nom.
<g>V + 도록:</g>Pour, pour que.

La première phrase mentionne qu'il n'y a pas de temps à consacrer à la vie culturelle, donc la deuxième phrase doit être remplie avec 4 pour avoir un lien.

Una de ellas son las novelas web, que son novelas cortas de Internet que se pueden leer en menos de 5 minutos. Esta novela está editada con interlineado amplio para que los ojos no se cansen incluso al leer en una pantalla de computadora. También se caracteriza por limitar el número de caracteres para que el contenido de la novela sea fácilmente comprensible.

--------------------
Люди говорят, что трудно выделить время для культурной жизни. В последнее время для таких людей появляются новые культурные содержания, которыми можно случайно наслаждаться в течение короткого периода времени. Один из них — веб-романы — короткие интернет-романы, которые можно прочитать менее чем за 5 минут. Этот роман отредактирован с широким межстрочным интервалом, чтобы глаза не уставали даже при чтении на экране компьютера. Для него также характерно ограничение количества персонажей, чтобы можно было легко понять содержание романа.

<g>V + 도록:</g>Для того, чтобы, чтобы.

1. Chame a atenção com conteúdos interessantes
2. Os personagens que aparecem no romance são pessoas modernas
3. Criado reescrevendo romances populares
4. Os leitores podem desfrutar em pouco tempo, sem qualquer pressão
--------------------
As pessoas dizem que é difícil reservar tempo para a vida cultural. Recentemente, estão surgindo novos conteúdos culturais que podem ser desfrutados casualmente por um curto período de tempo para essas pessoas. Um deles são os web novels, que são romances curtos da Internet que podem ser lidos em menos de 5 minutos. Este romance é editado com amplo espaçamento entre linhas para que os olhos não se cansem mesmo ao ler na tela do computador. Também se caracteriza por limitar o número de personagens para que o conteúdo do romance possa ser facilmente compreendido.

explain.hi:
1. दिलचस्प सामग्री से ध्यान आकर्षित करें
2. उपन्यास में दिखाई देने वाले पात्र आधुनिक लोग हैं
3. लोकप्रिय उपन्यासों को दोबारा लिखकर बनाया गया
4. पाठक बिना किसी दबाव के कम समय में आनंद ले सकते हैं
------------------------
लोग कहते हैं कि सांस्कृतिक जीवन के लिए समय निकालना कठिन है। हाल ही में, ऐसे लोगों के लिए नई सांस्कृतिक सामग्री उभर रही है जिसका आनंद थोड़े समय के लिए आकस्मिक रूप से लिया जा सकता है। उनमें से एक है वेब उपन्यास, जो छोटे इंटरनेट उपन्यास हैं जिन्हें 5 मिनट से भी कम समय में पढ़ा जा सकता है। इस उपन्यास को वाइड लाइन स्पेसिंग के साथ संपादित किया गया है ताकि कंप्यूटर स्क्रीन पर पढ़ते समय भी आंखें न थकें। इसकी विशेषता पात्रों की संख्या को सीमित करना भी है ताकि उपन्यास की सामग्री को आसानी से समझा जा सके।

<g>N + 을/를 위해(서), V + 기 위해(서)</g>: (के लिए), (क्रम में) के लिए।
<g>A/V + (으)ㄴ/는/(으)ㄹ + N:</g>संज्ञा संशोधक.
<g>V + 도록:</g>ताकि, ताकि.

पहले वाक्य में उल्लेख है कि सांस्कृतिक जीवन में बिताने के लिए समय नहीं है, इसलिए संबंध बनाने के लिए दूसरे वाक्य को 4 से भरना होगा।

explain.tr:
1. İlginç içeriklerle dikkat çekin
2. Romanda yer alan karakterler modern insanlardır
3. Popüler romanların yeniden yazılmasıyla oluşturulmuştur
4. Okuyucular herhangi bir baskı olmadan kısa sürede keyif alabilirler
--------------------
İnsanlar kültürel hayata zaman ayırmanın zor olduğunu söylüyor. Son zamanlarda bu tür insanlar için kısa süreliğine de olsa keyifle izlenebilecek yeni kültürel içerikler ortaya çıkıyor. Bunlardan biri, 5 dakikadan daha kısa sürede okunabilen kısa İnternet romanları olan web romanlarıdır. Bu roman, bilgisayar ekranında okurken bile gözlerin yorulmaması için geniş satır aralıklarıyla düzenlenmiştir. Ayrıca romanın içeriğinin kolayca anlaşılabilmesi için karakter sayısının sınırlandırılmasıyla da karakterize edilir.

content.q_text: 조선 시대에는 큰 명절이나 나라에 축하할 일이 생겼을 때 궁중에서 잔치를 열었다. 보통 잔치를 할 때에는 맛있고 귀한 음식을 가득 차려 놓고 성대하게 행사를 치렀다. 그러나 이 음식들은 잔치에 참석한 왕과 높은 지위의 일부 사람들만을 위한 것은 아니었다. 잔치가 끝난 뒤 가난한 백성들에게 나누어 주기 위해 일부러 많은 음식을 준비했던 것이다. 잔치를 통해 (   ) 의도에서였다.

explain.vi:
Vào thời đại Joseon, khi có ngày lễ lớn hoặc khi có việc cần chúc mừng, họ thường tổ chức tiệc trong cung điện. Thông thường khi tổ chức tiệc, họ chuẩn bị rất nhiều những món ăn ngon và quý cũng như tổ chức sự kiện thật hoành tráng. Tuy nhiên, những món ăn này không chỉ dành những người tham dự bữa tiệc như vua hay một số người có địa vị cao. Sau khi bữa tiệc kết thúc, họ còn đặc biệt chuẩn bị nhiều thức ăn để chia sẻ cho người dân nghèo. Thông qua bữa tiệc điều đó mang ý nghĩa ( ).

1. Để phô trương quyền lực của nhà vua
2. Để truyền bá các phong tục của triều đình
3. Để tiếp đón những người có địa vị cao
4. Hòa chung niềm vui của đất nước với đồng bào
--------------------
잔치: Tiệc
대접하다: Thiết đãi, tiếp đón

V + 기 위해(서): Để. Cấu trúc thể hiện mục đích.
V + (으)려고 하다: Định, sẽ làm gì đó.
explain.en:
1. To show of the king's power
2. To spread the customs of the court
3. To treat people of high status

--------------------
Selama Dinasti Joseon, ketika ada hari libur besar atau sesuatu untuk dirayakan di negara itu, sebuah pesta diadakan di istana. Biasanya, ketika sebuah pesta diadakan, itu diisi dengan makanan yang lezat dan berharga, dan upacara diadakan secara megah. Tetapi makanan ini tidak hanya untuk raja dan beberapa orang yang memiliki status tinggi yang menghadiri pesta itu. Setelah pesta selesai, mereka sengaja menyiapkan banyak makanan untuk dibagikan kepada orang miskin. Itu dimaksudkan untuk berbagi kegembiraan negara dengan orang-orang melalui pesta.

朝鮮王朝時代、国で大きな祝日やお祝い事があったとき、宮殿で饗宴が開かれました。通常、饗宴が開かれるとき、それは美味しくて貴重な食べ物で満たされ、儀式は盛大に行われました.しかし、これらの食べ物は、王様や饗宴に出席した一部の高位の人々のためだけのものではありませんでした.饗宴が終わった後、彼らは故意に貧しい人々に配るためにたくさんの食べ物を準備しました。饗宴を通じて、国の喜びを人々と分かち合うことを目的としていました。

2. Um die Bräuche des Gerichts zu verbreiten

--------------------
Während der Joseon-Dynastie, wenn es im Land einen großen Feiertag oder etwas zu feiern gab, wurde im Palast ein Fest gefeiert. Wenn ein Fest abgehalten wurde, war es normalerweise mit köstlichen und kostbaren Speisen gefüllt, und die Zeremonie wurde auf großartige Weise abgehalten. Aber diese Speisen waren nicht nur für den König und einige hochrangige Gäste bestimmt. Nachdem das Fest vorbei war, bereiteten sie absichtlich eine Menge Essen vor, um es an die Armen zu verteilen. Durch das Fest sollte die Freude des Landes mit den Menschen geteilt werden.

<g>잔치:</g>Fest, Party
<g>대접하다:</g>Empfangen, behandeln

Di solito, quando si teneva una festa, questa veniva riempita di cibo delizioso e prezioso, e la cerimonia si svolgeva in maniera grandiosa. 4. Делиться радостью страны с народом
--------------------
Во времена династии Чосон, когда в стране был большой праздник или что-то празднуемое, во дворце устраивали пир. Обычно, когда устраивалось застолье, оно было наполнено вкусной и драгоценной едой, а церемония проходила с размахом. Но эта еда предназначалась не только королю и некоторым высокопоставленным лицам, присутствовавшим на пиру. После того, как пир закончился, они специально приготовили много еды, чтобы раздать бедным. Целью праздника было поделиться с народом радостью страны.

4. देश की खुशियों को लोगों के साथ बाँटना
------------------------
जोसियन राजवंश के दौरान, जब देश में कोई बड़ी छुट्टी होती थी या कोई जश्न मनाने की बात होती थी, तो महल में एक दावत आयोजित की जाती थी। आमतौर पर, जब कोई दावत आयोजित की जाती थी, तो वह स्वादिष्ट और कीमती भोजन से भरी होती थी, और समारोह भव्य तरीके से आयोजित किया जाता था। लेकिन ये भोजन केवल राजा और दावत में शामिल हुए कुछ उच्च-प्रतिष्ठित लोगों के लिए नहीं थे। दावत ख़त्म होने के बाद, उन्होंने गरीबों को बाँटने के लिए जानबूझकर ढेर सारा खाना तैयार किया। इसका उद्देश्य दावत के माध्यम से देश की खुशियों को लोगों के साथ साझा करना था।

<g>잔치:</g>दावत, पार्टी
<g>대접하다:</g>प्राप्त करना, उपचार करना

<g>V + 기 위해(서)</g>: (के लिए), (क्रम में) के लिए।
<g>V + (으)려고 하다:</g>योजना बनाना, इरादा करना।




#######

1. To show of the king's power
2. To spread the customs of the court
3. To treat people of high status
4. To share the joy of the country with the people
--------------------
During the Joseon Dynasty, when there was a big holiday or something to celebrate in the country, a feast was held in the palace. Usually, when a feast was held, it was filled with delicious and precious food, and the ceremony was held in a grand way. But these foods were not just for the king and some of the high-status who attended the feast. After the feast was over, they purposely prepared a lot of food to distribute to the poor. It was intended to share the joy of the country with the people through the feast.

<g>잔치:</g> Feast, party
<g>대접하다:</g> To receive, to treat

<g>V + 기 위해(서)</g>: For (the sake of), (in order) to.
<g>V + (으)려고 하다:</g> To plan to, to intend to.
explain.tr:
1. Kralın gücünü göstermek
2. Mahkemenin geleneklerini yaymak
3. Yüksek statüdeki kişilere davranmak
4. Vatan sevincini halkla paylaşmak
--------------------
Joseon Hanedanlığı döneminde ülkede büyük bir bayram ya da kutlanacak bir şey olduğunda sarayda ziyafet düzenlenirdi. Genellikle bir ziyafet düzenlendiğinde, içi lezzetli ve kıymetli yiyeceklerle doldurulur ve tören görkemli bir şekilde yapılırdı. Ancak bu yiyecekler sadece krala ve ziyafete katılan bazı yüksek statülü kişilere yönelik değildi. Ziyafet bittikten sonra fakirlere dağıtmak üzere bilerek bol miktarda yiyecek hazırladılar. Bayram aracılığıyla ülke sevincinin halkla paylaşılması amaçlandı.

<g>잔치:</g>Bayram, parti
<g>대접하다:</g>Almak, tedavi etmek

<g>V + 기 위해(서)</g>: (Uğruna), (sırasıyla) için.
<g>V + (으)려고 하다:</g>Planlamak, niyet etmek.

--------------------

But these foods were not just for the king and some of the high-status who attended the feast. After the feast was over, they purposely prepared a lot of food to distribute to the poor. title: [28～31]다음을 읽고 (   )에 들어갈 내용으로 가장 알맞은 것을 고르십시오. (각 2점)

content.q_text: 최근에는 미술 작품을 과학적 연구의 참고 자료로 활용하고 있다. 그중 하나가 풍경화에 사용된 색을 연구하여 그 시대의 대기 상태를 알아보는 것이다. 일반적으로 공기 중에 먼지가 많으면 해가 질 때 하늘은 더 붉게 보인다고 한다. 그런데 산업화가 진행된 20세기 말 그림 속에 표현된 하늘이 다른 시대의 그림보다 더 붉다. 우리는 그 그림을 통해 20세기 말에 (   ) 사실을 확인할 수 있다.

explain.vi:
Gần đây, các tác phẩm nghệ thuật đang được sử dụng làm tài liệu tham khảo cho nghiên cứu khoa học. Một trong số đó là nghiên cứu màu sắc được sử dụng trong các bức tranh phong cảnh để tìm hiểu điều kiện khí quyển của thời đại đó. Thông thường, người ta nói rằng càng có nhiều bụi, bầu trời xuất hiện càng đỏ vào lúc hoàng hôn. Tuy nhiên, bầu trời được thể hiện trong các bức tranh vào cuối thế kỷ 20, khi công nghiệp hóa tiến bộ, có màu đỏ hơn so với các bức tranh từ các thời đại khác. Thông qua đó chúng ta có thể xác nhận được sự thật rằng cuối thế kỷ 20 ( ).

1. Bầu trời bị ô nhiễm bởi quá trình công nghiệp hóa
2. Có những nghiên cứu về trạng thái khí quyển
3. Các họa sĩ đã sử dụng các màu sắc khác nhau trong các bức tranh của họ
4. Các họa sĩ vẽ phong cảnh đã hoạt động rất năng nổ
--------------------
V + ㄴ/는 다고 하다: Câu trích dẫn gián tiếp.
explain.en:
1. The sky was polluted by industrialization
2. There were studies on the state of the atmosphere
3. Artists used different colors in their paintings
4. Landscape painters had been very active
--------------------
Recently, works of art are being used as reference materials for scientific research. One of them is to study the colors used in landscape paintings to find out the atmospheric conditions of that time. In general, the more dusty there is, the redder the sky appears at sunset. However, the sky expressed in paintings at the end of the 20th century, when industrialization progressed, is redder than in paintings from other eras. From the paintings, we can confirm that the sky was polluted by industrialization at the end of the 20th century.

<g>V + ㄴ/는 다고 하다:</g> Indirect speech.
explain.fr:
1. Le ciel a été pollué par l'industrialisation
2. Il y avait des études sur l'état de l'atmosphère
3. Les artistes ont utilisé différentes couleurs dans leurs peintures
4. Les paysagistes avaient été très actifs
--------------------
Récemment, des œuvres d'art sont utilisées comme matériaux de référence pour la recherche scientifique. L'un d'eux consiste à étudier les couleurs utilisées dans les peintures de paysages pour connaître les conditions atmosphériques de cette époque. En général, plus il y a de poussière, plus le ciel apparaît rouge au coucher du soleil. Cependant, le ciel exprimé dans les peintures de la fin du XXe siècle, lorsque l'industrialisation progresse, est plus rouge que dans les peintures d'autres époques. D'après les peintures, nous pouvons confirmer que le ciel a été pollué par l'industrialisation à la fin du 20ème siècle.

<g>V + ㄴ/는 다고 하다:</g>Discours indirect.


#######

1. The sky was polluted by industrialization
2. There were studies on the state of the atmosphere
3. Artists used different colors in their paintings
4. Landscape painters had been very active
--------------------
Recently, works of art are being used as reference materials for scientific research. One of them is to study the colors used in landscape paintings to find out the atmospheric conditions of that time. In general, the more dusty there is, the redder the sky appears at sunset. However, the sky expressed in paintings at the end of the 20th century, when industrialization progressed, is redder than in paintings from other eras. From the paintings, we can confirm that the sky was polluted by industrialization at the end of the 20th century.

explain.es:

2. Hubo estudios sobre el estado de la atmósfera
3. Los artistas usaron diferentes colores en sus pinturas
4. Los paisajistas habían sido muy activos
--------------------
Recientemente, las obras de arte se están utilizando como materiales de referencia para la investigación científica. Una de ellas es estudiar los colores utilizados en las pinturas de paisajes para conocer las condiciones atmosféricas de esa época. En general, cuanto más polvo hay, más rojo aparece el cielo al atardecer. Sin embargo, el cielo expresado en las pinturas de finales del siglo XX, cuando avanzaba la industrialización, es más rojo que en las pinturas de otras épocas. De las pinturas, podemos confirmar que el cielo fue contaminado por la industrialización a finales del siglo XX.

Akhir-akhir ini karya seni banyak digunakan sebagai bahan referensi untuk penelitian ilmiah. Salah satunya adalah mempelajari warna-warna yang digunakan dalam lukisan pemandangan untuk mengetahui kondisi atmosfer saat itu. Secara umum, semakin berdebu, langit tampak semakin merah saat matahari terbenam. Namun, langit yang diekspresikan dalam lukisan pada akhir abad ke-20, ketika industrialisasi berkembang, lebih merah daripada lukisan dari era lain. Dari lukisan-lukisan tersebut, kita dapat memastikan bahwa langit telah tercemar oleh industrialisasi pada akhir abad ke-20.

Jahrhunderts, als die Industrialisierung voranschritt, in Gemälden ausdrückte, röter als in Gemälden anderer Epochen. Anhand der Gemälde können wir bestätigen, dass der Himmel Ende des 20. Jahrhunderts durch die Industrialisierung verschmutzt wurde.

最近，艺术品被用作科学研究的参考资料。其中之一是研究山水画中使用的色彩，以了解当时的大气状况。一般来说，尘土越多，日落时的天空越红。然而，20世纪末工业化进程所表现的天空比其他时代的绘画更红。从这些画作中，我们可以确认天空在 20 世纪末被工业化污染了。

Uno di questi è studiare i colori usati nei dipinti di paesaggi per scoprire le condizioni atmosferiche di quel tempo. In generale, più c'è polvere, più rosso appare il cielo al tramonto. Dai dipinti possiamo confermare che il cielo era inquinato dall'industrializzazione alla fine del XX secolo.

ปัจจุบันมีการใช้ผลงานศิลปะเป็นวัสดุอ้างอิงในการวิจัยทางวิทยาศาสตร์ หนึ่งในนั้นคือการศึกษาสีที่ใช้ในการวาดภาพทิวทัศน์เพื่อหาสภาพบรรยากาศในขณะนั้น โดยทั่วไป ยิ่งมีฝุ่นมาก ท้องฟ้าก็ยิ่งแดงมากขึ้นเมื่อพระอาทิตย์ตกดิน อย่างไรก็ตาม ท้องฟ้าที่แสดงออกมาในภาพวาดในช่วงปลายศตวรรษที่ 20 เมื่อการพัฒนาทางอุตสาหกรรมก้าวหน้าไป มีสีแดงมากกว่าในภาพวาดจากยุคอื่นๆ จากภาพวาดเราสามารถยืนยันได้ว่าท้องฟ้าถูกปนเปื้อนจากการพัฒนาอุตสาหกรรมเมื่อปลายศตวรรษที่ 20

#######

1. The sky was polluted by industrialization
2. There were studies on the state of the atmosphere
3. Artists used different colors in their paintings
4. Landscape painters had been very active
--------------------
Recently, works of art are being used as reference materials for scientific research. One of them is to study the colors used in landscape paintings to find out the atmospheric conditions of that time. In general, the more dusty there is, the redder the sky appears at sunset. However, the sky expressed in paintings at the end of the 20th century, when industrialization progressed, is redder than in paintings from other eras. From the paintings, we can confirm that the sky was polluted by industrialization at the end of the 20th century.

<g>V + ㄴ/는 다고 하다:</g> Indirect speech.
explain.pt:
1. O céu foi poluído pela industrialização
2. Houve estudos sobre o estado da atmosfera
3. Artistas usaram cores diferentes em suas pinturas
4. Os pintores paisagistas eram muito ativos
--------------------
Recentemente, obras de arte têm sido utilizadas como materiais de referência para pesquisas científicas. Uma delas é estudar as cores utilizadas nas pinturas de paisagens para conhecer as condições atmosféricas da época. Em geral, quanto mais poeira houver, mais vermelho ficará o céu ao pôr do sol. No entanto, o céu expresso nas pinturas do final do século XX, quando a industrialização avançava, é mais vermelho do que nas pinturas de outras épocas. Pelas pinturas podemos confirmar que o céu foi poluído pela industrialização no final do século XX.

explain.hi:
1. औद्योगीकरण से आकाश प्रदूषित हो गया
2. वातावरण की स्थिति पर अध्ययन हुए
3. कलाकारों ने अपनी पेंटिंग्स में अलग-अलग रंगों का इस्तेमाल किया
4. लैंडस्केप चित्रकार बहुत सक्रिय थे
------------------------
हाल ही में, कला के कार्यों को वैज्ञानिक अनुसंधान के लिए संदर्भ सामग्री के रूप में उपयोग किया जा रहा है। उनमें से एक है उस समय की वायुमंडलीय स्थितियों का पता लगाने के लिए लैंडस्केप पेंटिंग में इस्तेमाल किए गए रंगों का अध्ययन करना। सामान्य तौर पर, जितनी अधिक धूल होगी, सूर्यास्त के समय आकाश उतना ही अधिक लाल दिखाई देगा। हालाँकि, 20वीं सदी के अंत में, जब औद्योगिकीकरण आगे बढ़ा, चित्रों में व्यक्त आकाश अन्य युगों के चित्रों की तुलना में अधिक लाल है। चित्रों से हम इस बात की पुष्टि कर सकते हैं कि 20वीं सदी के अंत में औद्योगीकरण के कारण आकाश प्रदूषित हो गया था।

explain.tr:
1. Gökyüzü sanayileşme nedeniyle kirlendi
2. Atmosferin durumuyla ilgili çalışmalar yapıldı
3. Sanatçılar resimlerinde farklı renkler kullanmışlardır
4. Manzara ressamları çok aktifti
--------------------
Son dönemde sanat eserleri bilimsel araştırmalarda referans materyal olarak kullanılıyor. Bunlardan biri de manzara resimlerinde kullanılan renkleri inceleyerek o dönemin atmosferik koşullarını öğrenmektir. Genel olarak, ne kadar tozlu olursa, gün batımında gökyüzü o kadar kırmızı görünür. Ancak sanayileşmenin ilerlediği 20. yüzyılın sonlarında resimlerde ifade edilen gökyüzü, diğer dönemlere ait resimlere göre daha kırmızıdır. Resimlerden 20. yüzyılın sonlarında sanayileşme nedeniyle gökyüzünün kirlendiğini doğrulayabiliriz.

content.q_text: 어떤 일의 순서를 정할 때 사람들은 흔히 ‘가위바위보’를 이용한다. 그런데 사람들이 아무 생각 없이 ‘가위바위보’를 하는 것 같지만 자세히 관찰해 보면 (   )발견할 수 있다. 즉, 이긴 사람은 자신이 선택한 것을 그대로 유지하는 반면에 진 사람은 다른 것을 선택하는 경우가 많다. 이것은 사람들이 순간적인 행동을 할 때도 이기려는 본능으로 자신에게 유리하게 반응한다는 것을 보여 준다.

explain.vi:
Mọi người thường sử dụng trò 'Oẳn tù tì' khi quyết định thứ tự của một việc nào đó. Tuy nhiên, họ có vẻ đang chơi oẳn tù tì mà không suy nghĩ gì nhưng nếu quan sát kỹ thì có thể phát hiện ra (   ). Nói cách khác, người chiến thắng giữ nguyên sự lựa chọn của mình trong khi người thua cuộc thường chọn thứ khác. Điều này cho thấy mọi người sẽ phản ứng có lợi cho bản thân theo bản năng chiến thắng ngay cả khi họ hành động nhất thời.
1. Có nhiều loại khác nhau
2. Có khuynh hướng giống nhau
3. Có tần xuất thống nhất
4. Có điều kiện cần thiết
--------------------
가위바위보: Trò oẳn tù tì, kéo búa bao
아무 생각 없이: Không chút suy nghĩ, vô tình

V + (으)ㄴ다는 것을 보여 주다: Cho thấy điều gì.
explain.en:
1. There are various types.
2. There is a certain tendency
3. The frequency is the same
4. There are necessary conditions
--------------------
People often use the game of rock-paper-scissors when deciding the order of something. However, although it seems that people do 'rock, paper, scissors' without thinking, if you look closely, you can find that there is a certain tendency. In other words, winners keep their choice while losers usually choose something else. This suggests that people will instinctively respond in their favor even if they act momentarily.

<g>가위바위보:</g> Rock-paper-scissors
<g>아무 생각 없이:</g> With no definite idea, unintentionally

<g>V + (으)ㄴ다는 것을 보여 주다:</g> Indicate something.
explain.fr:
1. Il existe différents types.
2. Il y a une certaine tendance
3. La fréquence est la même
4. Il y a des conditions nécessaires
--------------------
Les gens utilisent souvent le jeu pierre-papier-ciseaux pour décider de l'ordre de quelque chose. Cependant, bien qu'il semble que les gens fassent « pierre, papier, ciseaux » sans réfléchir, si vous regardez attentivement, vous pouvez constater qu'il existe une certaine tendance. En d'autres termes, les gagnants gardent leur choix tandis que les perdants choisissent généralement autre chose. Cela suggère que les gens répondront instinctivement en leur faveur même s'ils agissent momentanément.

<g>가위바위보:</g>Pierre papier ciseaux
<g>아무 생각 없이:</g>Sans idée précise, involontairement

3. La frecuencia es la misma

--------------------
La gente a menudo usa el juego de piedra, papel o tijera para decidir el orden de algo. Sin embargo, aunque parece que la gente hace 'piedra, papel o tijera' sin pensar, si te fijas bien, puedes encontrar que hay una cierta tendencia. En otras palabras, los ganadores conservan su elección mientras que los perdedores suelen elegir otra cosa. Esto sugiere que las personas responderán instintivamente a su favor incluso si actúan momentáneamente.

Orang sering menggunakan permainan batu-kertas-gunting ketika memutuskan urutan sesuatu. Другими словами, победители сохраняют свой выбор, а проигравшие обычно выбирают что-то другое. content.q_text: 아이스크림의 부드러움을 결정짓는 중요한 요소는 바로 공기이다. 숙성된 아이스크림 원료에 공기를 주입하면 부피가 점점 늘어나면서 조직이 부드러워지게 된다. 즉, 불어넣은 공기의 비율이 높을수록 부드러운 아이스크림이 되는 것이다. 그런데 한번 녹은 아이스크림은 다시 얼리더라도 이전처럼 부드러워지지 않는다. 원료에 숨어 있던 (    ) 딱딱한 얼음 결정이 생기게 되어 부드러운 맛이 사라지는 것이다.

explain.vi:
Yếu tố quan trọng quyết định độ mềm của kem là không khí. Nếu bơm không khí vào nguyên liệu kem đã được ủ, khối lượng sẽ từ từ tăng lên và các mô kem sẽ trở nên mềm hơn. Nói cách khác, tỷ lệ không khí được đưa vào càng cao thì kem càng mềm. Tuy nhiên, một khi kem đã tan chảy sẽ không còn mềm như trước nữa, ngay cả khi đông lại. Khi không khí ẩn giấu trong nguyên liệu (   ), các tinh thể đá cứng xuất hiện, lúc đó cảm giác mềm của kem sẽ biến mất.
1. Thoát ra ngoài không khí
2. Khối lượng của không khí tăng
3. Điều chỉnh sau tỷ lệ của không khí
4. Không khí làm nguyên liệu mềm ra
--------------------
부피: Thể tích, khối lượng
주입하다: Rót, truyền, bơm vào
불어넣다: Thối, đưa vào
explain.en:
1. The air (hidden in the raw material) escapes
2. The volume of air (hidden in the raw material) increases
3. By adjusting the ratio of the air
4. The air (hidden in the raw material) softens the raw material
--------------------
The important factor determining the softness of ice cream is air. If air is injected into the annealed ice cream material, the volume will slowly increase and the cream tissues will become softer. In other words, the higher the percentage of air that is introduced, the softer the ice cream. However, once the ice cream has melted it will not be as soft as before, even when frozen. As the air hidden in the raw material escapes, hard ice crystals form and the soft taste disappears.

<g>부피:</g> Volume, bulk
<g>주입하다:</g> To pour, to inject, to cram
<g>불어넣다:</g> To inspire, to imbue, to instill.
explain.fr:
1. L'air (caché dans la matière première) s'échappe
2. Le volume d'air (caché dans la matière première) augmente
3. En ajustant le rapport de l'air
4. L'air (caché dans la matière première) ramollit la matière première
--------------------
Le facteur important déterminant la douceur de la crème glacée est l'air. Si de l'air est injecté dans le matériau de crème glacée recuit, le volume augmentera lentement et les tissus de la crème deviendront plus mous. En d'autres termes, plus le pourcentage d'air introduit est élevé, plus la glace est molle. Cependant, une fois la crème glacée fondue, elle ne sera plus aussi molle qu'avant, même lorsqu'elle est congelée. Au fur et à mesure que l'air caché dans la matière première s'échappe, des cristaux de glace durs se forment et le goût doux disparaît.

<g>부피:</g>Volume, vrac
<g>주입하다:</g>Verser, injecter, fourrer
<g>불어넣다:</g>Inspirer, imprégner, instiller.

#######

--------------------

In other words, the higher the percentage of air that is introduced, the softer the ice cream. However, once the ice cream has melted it will not be as soft as before, even when frozen. explain.es:
1. El aire (oculto en la materia prima) se escapa
2. El volumen de aire (oculto en la materia prima) aumenta
3. Ajustando la proporción del aire
4. El aire (oculto en la materia prima) suaviza la materia prima
--------------------
El factor importante que determina la suavidad del helado es el aire. Si se inyecta aire en el material de helado recocido, el volumen aumentará lentamente y los tejidos de la crema se volverán más suaves. Es decir, cuanto mayor sea el porcentaje de aire que se introduzca, más suave será el helado. Sin embargo, una vez que el helado se haya derretido, no estará tan suave como antes, incluso cuando esté congelado. A medida que escapa el aire oculto en la materia prima, se forman cristales de hielo duro y desaparece el sabor suave.

<g>부피:</g>Volumen, a granel
<g>주입하다:</g>Verter, inyectar, abarrotar
<g>불어넣다:</g>Inspirar, imbuir, inculcar.

1. Udara (tersembunyi dalam bahan mentah) keluar
2. Volume udara (tersembunyi dalam bahan baku) meningkat
3. Dengan menyesuaikan rasio udara
4. Udara (tersembunyi dalam bahan baku) melembutkan bahan mentah
--------------------
Faktor penting yang menentukan kelembutan es krim adalah udara. Jika udara disuntikkan ke dalam bahan es krim yang dianil, volumenya akan meningkat perlahan dan jaringan krim akan menjadi lebih lembut. Dengan kata lain, semakin tinggi persentase udara yang dimasukkan, semakin lembut es krimnya. Namun, begitu es krim meleleh, es krimnya tidak akan selembut sebelumnya, bahkan saat dibekukan. Saat udara yang tersembunyi di bahan mentah keluar, kristal es yang keras terbentuk dan rasa lembutnya menghilang.

1. 原料に隠れていた空気が抜ける
2. 空気（原料に隠れている）の量が増える
3. 空気の比率を調整することで
4.空気（原料に隠れている）が原料を柔らかくする
--------------------
アイスクリームの柔らかさを決める重要な要素は空気です。アニールされたアイスクリーム材料に空気を注入すると、体積が徐々に増加し、クリームの組織が柔らかくなります。つまり、導入される空気の割合が高いほど、アイスクリームは柔らかくなります。ただし、アイスクリームが溶けてしまうと、冷凍しても柔らかくなりません。原料に隠れていた空気が抜けることで、固い氷の結晶ができ、やわらかな味わいが消えていきます。

<g>부피:</g>ボリューム、バルク
<g>주입하다:</g>注ぐ、注入する、詰め込む
<g>불어넣다:</g>鼓舞する、吹き込む、浸透させる。

2. Das (im Rohmaterial verborgene) Luftvolumen nimmt zu
3. Durch Einstellen des Luftverhältnisses
4. Die (im Rohmaterial verborgene) Luft erweicht das Rohmaterial
--------------------
Der wichtige Faktor, der die Weichheit von Eiscreme bestimmt, ist Luft. Wenn Luft in das getemperte Eiscremematerial eingeblasen wird, nimmt das Volumen langsam zu und das Sahnegewebe wird weicher. Mit anderen Worten, je höher der Anteil an eingebrachter Luft ist, desto weicher ist die Eiscreme. Sobald das Eis jedoch geschmolzen ist, ist es nicht mehr so ​​weich wie zuvor, selbst wenn es gefroren ist. Durch das Entweichen der im Rohmaterial verborgenen Luft bilden sich harte Eiskristalle und der weiche Geschmack verschwindet.

<g>부피:</g>Volumen, Masse
<g>주입하다:</g>Gießen, spritzen, stopfen
<g>불어넣다:</g>Inspirieren, durchtränken, einflößen.

1.空气（隐藏在原料中）逸出
2.空气（隐藏在原料中）的体积增加
3.通过调整空气的比例
4.空气（隐藏在原料中）软化原料
--------------------
决定冰淇淋柔软度的重要因素是空气。如果将空气注入退火的冰淇淋材料中，体积会慢慢增加，奶油组织会变得更柔软。换句话说，引入的空气百分比越高，冰淇淋就越软。然而，一旦冰淇淋融化，它就不会像以前那样柔软了，即使是冷冻的。随着隐藏在原料中的空气逸出，形成坚硬的冰晶，柔软的味道消失。

<g>부피:</g>体积，散装
<g>주입하다:</g>倾倒，注入，填塞

#######

1. The air (hidden in the raw material) escapes
2. The volume of air (hidden in the raw material) increases
3. By adjusting the ratio of the air
4. The air (hidden in the raw material) softens the raw material
--------------------
The important factor determining the softness of ice cream is air. If air is injected into the annealed ice cream material, the volume will slowly increase and the cream tissues will become softer. In other words, the higher the percentage of air that is introduced, the softer the ice cream. However, once the ice cream has melted it will not be as soft as before, even when frozen. As the air hidden in the raw material escapes, hard ice crystals form and the soft taste disappears.

--------------------
Il fattore importante che determina la morbidezza del gelato è l'aria. Se l'aria viene iniettata nel materiale del gelato ricotto, il volume aumenterà lentamente e i tessuti della crema diventeranno più morbidi. In altre parole, maggiore è la percentuale di aria immessa, più soffice sarà il gelato. Tuttavia, una volta che il gelato si sarà sciolto, non sarà più morbido come prima, anche se congelato. Man mano che l'aria nascosta nella materia prima fuoriesce, si formano cristalli di ghiaccio duro e il gusto morbido scompare.

O fator importante que determina a maciez do sorvete é o ar. Se for injetado ar no material recozido do sorvete, o volume aumentará lentamente e os tecidos do creme ficarão mais macios. Ou seja, quanto maior for a percentagem de ar introduzida, mais macio será o gelado. No entanto, uma vez derretido, o sorvete não ficará tão macio como antes, mesmo quando congelado. À medida que o ar escondido na matéria-prima escapa, formam-se cristais de gelo duros e o sabor suave desaparece.

3. वायु के अनुपात को समायोजित करके
4. वायु (कच्चे माल में छिपी) कच्चे माल को नरम कर देती है
------------------------
आइसक्रीम की कोमलता का निर्धारण करने वाला महत्वपूर्ण कारक हवा है। यदि हवा को एनील्ड आइसक्रीम सामग्री में इंजेक्ट किया जाता है, तो मात्रा धीरे-धीरे बढ़ेगी और क्रीम ऊतक नरम हो जाएंगे। दूसरे शब्दों में, हवा का प्रतिशत जितना अधिक होगा, आइसक्रीम उतनी ही नरम होगी। हालाँकि, एक बार आइसक्रीम पिघल जाने पर यह पहले जितनी नरम नहीं रहेगी, जमने पर भी। जैसे ही कच्चे माल में छिपी हवा बाहर निकलती है, कठोर बर्फ के क्रिस्टल बन जाते हैं और नरम स्वाद गायब हो जाता है।

title: [28～31]다음을 읽고 ( )에 들어갈 내용으로 가장 알맞은 것을 고르십시오. (각 2점)

content.q_text: 인간은 (    ) 중심으로 세상일을 받아들인다. 연인과 헤어진 뒤 듣는 노래 가사는 전부 자신의 이야기인 양 생각하고, 어쩌다 머리 모양이 마음에 안 드는 날은 모두 자기만 쳐다보는 것처럼 느끼기도 한다. 고등학생은 이 세상에 자기들만 사는 것처럼 행동하고, 아이를 임신한 사람은 길거리에서 유난히 임신부만 눈에 들어온다. 이렇듯 우리는 세상의 많은 사건들에 자신을 투영해 유사성을 발견하고 이에 공감하며 살아간다.

explain.vi:
Con người lấy (   ) làm trung tâm và tiếp nhận mọi việc trên đời này. Một người cho rằng lời bài hát mình nghe sau khi chia tay người yêu đều là câu chuyện của bản thân, hoặc vào những ngày không thích kiểu tóc của mình họ sẽ có cảm giác như mọi người đang nhìn mình. Học sinh trung học hành động như thể họ là người duy nhất sống trên thế giới này, và những người mang thai chỉ đặc biệt chú ý tới những phụ nữ mang thai khác trên đường phố. Như vậy, chúng ta tự phản chiếu bản thân mình vào nhiều sự kiện trên thế giới để phát hiện ra sự tương đồng rồi đồng cảm và sống với điều này.
1. Bản thân mình
2. Sự kiện trên thế giới
3. Việc mắt mình nhìn thấy 
4. Người có cùng lập trường
--------------------
눈에 들어온다: Bày ra trước mắt, thu vào tầm mắt
임신부: Phụ nữ mang thai
explain.en:
1. Themselves
2. Worldly events
3. What they see
4. Those who are in the same position.
--------------------
Human beings accept the affairs of the world around themselves. A person thinks that the lyrics they listen to after breaking up with their lover are all their own stories, or on days when they don't like their hairstyle they will feel like people are looking at them. High school students act as if they are the only people living in this world, and pregnant people only pay special attention to other pregnant women on the street. As such, we mirror ourselves into many events in the world to spot similarities and then empathize and live with this.

<g>눈에 들어온다:</g> To jump to the eyes 
<g>임신부:</g> Pregnant women.
explain.fr:
1. Eux-mêmes
2. Événements mondains
3. Ce qu'ils voient
4. Ceux qui sont dans la même position.
--------------------
Les êtres humains acceptent les affaires du monde autour d'eux. Une personne pense que les paroles qu'elle écoute après avoir rompu avec son amant sont toutes ses propres histoires, ou les jours où elle n'aime pas sa coiffure, elle aura l'impression que les gens la regardent. Les élèves du secondaire agissent comme s'ils étaient les seules personnes vivant dans ce monde, et les femmes enceintes ne prêtent une attention particulière qu'aux autres femmes enceintes dans la rue. En tant que tel, nous nous reflétons dans de nombreux événements dans le monde pour repérer les similitudes, puis sympathiser et vivre avec cela.

<g>눈에 들어온다:</g>A sauter aux yeux
<g>임신부:</g>Femmes enceintes.

Manusia menerima urusan dunia di sekitar diri mereka sendiri. Seseorang berpikir bahwa lirik yang mereka dengarkan setelah putus dengan kekasihnya adalah cerita mereka sendiri, atau pada hari-hari ketika mereka tidak menyukai gaya rambut mereka, mereka akan merasa seperti orang-orang melihat mereka. Siswa sekolah menengah bertindak seolah-olah mereka adalah satu-satunya orang yang hidup di dunia ini, dan orang hamil hanya memberi perhatian khusus pada wanita hamil lainnya di jalanan. Dengan demikian, kita mencerminkan diri kita sendiri ke dalam banyak peristiwa di dunia untuk menemukan kesamaan dan kemudian berempati dan hidup dengan ini.

3. 彼らが見るもの
4.同じ立場の方。
--------------------
人間は自分の周りの世界の出来事を受け入れます。恋人と別れた後に聞く歌詞はすべて自分の話だと思ったり、髪型が気に入らない日は人に見られているように感じたりします。高校生は自分だけがこの世に生きているかのように振る舞い、妊婦は路上で他の妊婦にのみ特別な注意を払います。そのため、私たちは自分自身を世界の多くの出来事に映し出し、類似点を見つけて共感し、これと一緒に暮らしています.

<g>눈에 들어온다:</g>目に飛び込む
<g>임신부:</g>妊娠中の女性。

#######

1. Themselves
2. Worldly events
3. What they see
4. Those who are in the same position.
--------------------
Human beings accept the affairs of the world around themselves. A person thinks that the lyrics they listen to after breaking up with their lover are all their own stories, or on days when they don't like their hairstyle they will feel like people are looking at them. High school students act as if they are the only people living in this world, and pregnant people only pay special attention to other pregnant women on the street. As such, we mirror ourselves into many events in the world to spot similarities and then empathize and live with this.

<g>눈에 들어온다:</g> To jump to the eyes 
<g>임신부:</g> Pregnant women.
explain.de:
1. Sich selbst
2. Weltliche Ereignisse
3. Was sie sehen
4. Diejenigen, die in der gleichen Position sind.
--------------------
Die Menschen akzeptieren die Angelegenheiten der Welt um sich herum. Eine Person denkt, dass die Texte, die sie sich nach der Trennung von ihrem Geliebten anhören, ihre eigenen Geschichten sind, oder dass sie an Tagen, an denen sie ihre Frisur nicht mögen, das Gefühl haben, dass die Leute sie ansehen. Gymnasiasten tun so, als wären sie die einzigen Menschen auf dieser Welt, und Schwangere schenken anderen Schwangeren auf der Straße besondere Aufmerksamkeit. Als solche spiegeln wir uns in vielen Ereignissen auf der Welt, um Ähnlichkeiten zu erkennen und uns dann einzufühlen und damit zu leben.

<g>눈에 들어온다:</g>Zu den Augen springen
<g>임신부:</g>Schwangere Frau.

2. Eventi mondani

--------------------
Gli esseri umani accettano gli affari del mondo che li circonda. Una persona pensa che i testi che ascolta dopo aver rotto con il proprio amante siano tutte le sue storie, o nei giorni in cui non gli piace la sua pettinatura si sentirà come se la gente la stesse guardando. Gli studenti delle scuole superiori si comportano come se fossero le uniche persone che vivono in questo mondo e le persone incinte prestano particolare attenzione solo alle altre donne incinte per strada. Pertanto, ci rispecchiamo in molti eventi nel mondo per individuare somiglianze e quindi entrare in empatia e vivere con questo.

--------------------
Люди принимают дела окружающего мира. Человек думает, что тексты песен, которые он слушает после расставания с возлюбленным, — это все его собственные истории, или в те дни, когда ему не нравится его прическа, ему будет казаться, что люди смотрят на него. Старшеклассники ведут себя так, как будто они единственные люди, живущие в этом мире, а беременные обращают особое внимание только на других беременных женщин на улице. Таким образом, мы отражаем себя во многих событиях в мире, чтобы обнаружить сходство, а затем сопереживать и жить с этим.

มนุษย์ยอมรับเรื่องราวต่างๆ ของโลกรอบตัวพวกเขาเอง คนๆ หนึ่งคิดว่าเนื้อเพลงที่พวกเขาฟังหลังจากเลิกกับคนรักนั้นเป็นเรื่องราวของตัวเองทั้งหมด หรือในวันที่พวกเขาไม่ชอบทรงผมของพวกเขา พวกเขาจะรู้สึกเหมือนมีคนมองพวกเขาอยู่ นักเรียนมัธยมปลายทำตัวราวกับว่าพวกเขาเป็นเพียงคนเดียวที่อาศัยอยู่ในโลกนี้ และคนท้องก็ให้ความสนใจเป็นพิเศษกับสตรีมีครรภ์คนอื่นๆ ที่อยู่บนถนนเท่านั้น ด้วยเหตุนี้ เราจึงสะท้อนตัวเองเข้าไปในเหตุการณ์ต่างๆ มากมายในโลกเพื่อมองเห็นความคล้ายคลึงกัน จากนั้นจึงเห็นอกเห็นใจและดำเนินชีวิตร่วมกับสิ่งนี้

Os seres humanos aceitam os assuntos do mundo ao seu redor. Uma pessoa pensa que as letras que ouve depois de terminar com o amante são todas histórias suas, ou nos dias em que não gosta do penteado vai sentir que as pessoas estão olhando para ela. Os estudantes do ensino médio agem como se fossem as únicas pessoas que vivem neste mundo, e as grávidas só prestam atenção especial às outras grávidas na rua. Como tal, espelhamo-nos em muitos eventos no mundo para identificar semelhanças e depois ter empatia e viver com isso.

------------------------
मनुष्य अपने आस-पास की दुनिया के मामलों को स्वीकार करता है। एक व्यक्ति सोचता है कि जो गीत वह अपने प्रेमी से अलग होने के बाद सुनता है वह सब उसकी अपनी कहानियाँ हैं, या जिस दिन उसे अपना हेयरस्टाइल पसंद नहीं आएगा तो उसे ऐसा लगेगा जैसे लोग उसे देख रहे हैं। हाई स्कूल के छात्र ऐसे व्यवहार करते हैं मानो वे ही इस दुनिया में रहने वाले एकमात्र व्यक्ति हों, और गर्भवती लोग केवल सड़क पर अन्य गर्भवती महिलाओं पर विशेष ध्यान देते हैं। इस प्रकार, हम समानताएं पहचानने के लिए दुनिया की कई घटनाओं में खुद को प्रतिबिंबित करते हैं और फिर सहानुभूति रखते हैं और इसके साथ जीते हैं।

<g>임신부:</g>प्रेग्नेंट औरत।

Lise öğrencileri bu dünyada sadece kendileri yaşıyormuş gibi davranıyor, hamileler ise sadece sokaktaki diğer hamile kadınlara özel ilgi gösteriyor. Bu nedenle, benzerlikleri tespit etmek için dünyadaki birçok olaya kendimizi yansıtır, sonra empati kurar ve bununla yaşarız.

content.q_text: 최근 ‘자서전 쓰기’강좌를 듣는 70∼80대 노년층이 늘고 있다. 그들은 자서전이 유명한 인물의 일대기라는 고정관념에서 벗어나 자신의 지나간 시간에 대한 기록을 남기고자 하는 것이다. 강좌를 들으면서 자서전을 쓰려고 하는 이 노인들은 한국 전쟁을 겪고 오늘날의 눈부신 경제 성장을 이루어 낸 주역이자 산 증인이다. 따라서 이들의 자서전은 그저 ‘이 세상을 살다 간 흔적’이 아니라 (    ) 중요한 기록이 될 것이다.

explain.vi:
Gần đây, ngày càng có nhiều ở tầng lớp cao niên có độ tuổi từ 70 đến 80 tham gia khóa học 'Viết tự truyện'. Họ muốn thoát khỏi định kiến ​​rằng tự truyện là tiểu sử của một người nổi tiếng, và muốn ghi chép lại quá khứ của chính họ. Những người cao tuổi vừa tham gia khóa học, vừa viết tự truyện này chính là nhân chứng sống, là nhân vật chính đã trải qua cuộc chiến tranh Triều Tiên và tạo ra sự tăng trưởng kinh tế vượt bậc ngày hôm nay. Vì vậy tự chuyện của họ không chỉ là 'dấu vết của sự sống trên thế giới này' mà còn là tài liệu ghi chép quan trọng (   ) .
1. Sự tự nhìn lại cuộc sống
2. Về quá trình phát triển của nhân vật
3. Thể hiện quan điểm sống và triết lý của mỗi cá nhân
4. Về lịch sử hiện đại và quá trình công nghiệp hóa của Hàn Quốc
--------------------
일대기: Nhật ký đời người, tiểu sử
고정관념: Định kiến, quan niệm cố hữu
눈부시다: Chói lòa, sáng chói
현대사: Lịch sử hiện đại
explain.en:
1. Of reflecting on their lives
2. Of the character's growth
3. That shows the individual's outlook on life and philosophy
4. Of Korea's modern history and industrialization process
--------------------
Recently, more and more seniors aged between 70 and 80 are taking the 'Autobiography Writing' course. They wanted to break free from the stereotype that an autobiography was a biography of a famous person, and wanted to document their own past. The elderly people who attended this course and wrote their autobiographies are living witnesses and protagonists who went through the Korean War and created the phenomenal economic growth today. Therefore, their autobiography will be an important record of Korea's modern history and industrialization process, not just 'the traces of living in this world'.

<g>일대기:</g> Diary of human life, biography
<g>고정관념:</g> Stereotype, fixed idea
<g>눈부시다:</g> To be dazzling, to be bright
<g>현대사:</g> Modern history.
explain.fr:
1. De réfléchir sur leur vie
2. De la croissance du personnage
3. Cela montre la vision de l'individu sur la vie et la philosophie
4. De l'histoire moderne de la Corée et du processus d'industrialisation
--------------------
Depuis peu, de plus en plus de seniors âgés de 70 à 80 ans suivent le cours 'Autobiography Writing'. Ils voulaient se libérer du stéréotype selon lequel une autobiographie était la biographie d'une personne célèbre et voulaient documenter leur propre passé. Les personnes âgées qui ont suivi ce cours et écrit leurs autobiographies sont des témoins vivants et des protagonistes qui ont traversé la guerre de Corée et créé la croissance économique phénoménale d'aujourd'hui. Par conséquent, leur autobiographie sera un témoignage important de l'histoire moderne de la Corée et du processus d'industrialisation, et pas seulement « les traces de la vie dans ce monde ».

<g>일대기:</g>Journal de la vie humaine, biographie
<g>고정관념:</g>Stéréotype, idée fixe
<g>눈부시다:</g>Être éblouissant, être brillant
<g>현대사:</g>Histoire moderne.

#######

1. Of reflecting on their lives
2. Of the character's growth
3. That shows the individual's outlook on life and philosophy
4. Of Korea's modern history and industrialization process
--------------------
Recently, more and more seniors aged between 70 and 80 are taking the 'Autobiography Writing' course. They wanted to break free from the stereotype that an autobiography was a biography of a famous person, and wanted to document their own past. The elderly people who attended this course and wrote their autobiographies are living witnesses and protagonists who went through the Korean War and created the phenomenal economic growth today. Therefore, their autobiography will be an important record of Korea's modern history and industrialization process, not just 'the traces of living in this world'.

<g>일대기:</g> Diary of human life, biography
<g>고정관념:</g> Stereotype, fixed idea
<g>눈부시다:</g> To be dazzling, to be bright
<g>현대사:</g> Modern history.
explain.es:
1. De reflexionar sobre sus vidas
2. Del crecimiento del personaje
3. Que muestre la perspectiva del individuo sobre la vida y la filosofía.
4. Sobre la historia moderna y el proceso de industrialización de Corea
--------------------
Recientemente, más y más personas mayores de entre 70 y 80 años están tomando el curso de 'Escritura de autobiografía'. Querían romper con el estereotipo de que una autobiografía era una biografía de una persona famosa y querían documentar su propio pasado. Las personas mayores que asistieron a este curso y escribieron sus autobiografías son testigos vivos y protagonistas que atravesaron la Guerra de Corea y crearon el fenomenal crecimiento económico actual. Por lo tanto, su autobiografía será un registro importante de la historia moderna y el proceso de industrialización de Corea, no solo 'las huellas de vivir en este mundo'.

<g>일대기:</g>Diario de la vida humana, biografía.
<g>고정관념:</g>Estereotipo, idea fija

explain.id:
1. Merefleksikan kehidupan mereka
2. Dari pertumbuhan karakter
3. Itu menunjukkan pandangan individu tentang kehidupan dan filosofi
4. Sejarah modern dan proses industrialisasi Korea
--------------------
Baru-baru ini, semakin banyak manula berusia antara 70 dan 80 mengikuti kursus 'Menulis Autobiografi'. Mereka ingin melepaskan diri dari stereotip bahwa otobiografi adalah biografi orang terkenal, dan ingin mendokumentasikan masa lalu mereka sendiri. Orang-orang tua yang menghadiri kursus ini dan menulis otobiografi mereka adalah saksi hidup dan protagonis yang melalui Perang Korea dan menciptakan pertumbuhan ekonomi yang fenomenal saat ini. Oleh karena itu, otobiografi mereka akan menjadi catatan penting tentang sejarah modern dan proses industrialisasi Korea, bukan hanya 'jejak kehidupan di dunia ini'.

<g>일대기:</g>Buku harian kehidupan manusia, biografi
<g>고정관념:</g>Stereotip, ide tetap
<g>눈부시다:</g>Untuk menjadi mempesona, menjadi cerah
<g>현대사:</g>Sejarah modern.

explain.ja:
1. 自分の人生を振り返る
2.キャラクターの成長について
3. 個人の人生観や哲学を示すもの
4. 韓国の近代史と産業化の過程
--------------------
最近では70～80歳代の高齢者が「自伝執筆」コースを受講する人が増えています。彼らは、自伝が有名人の伝記であるという固定観念から抜け出し、自分の過去を記録したいと考えていました。このコースに参加して自伝を書いた高齢者は、朝鮮戦争を経験し、今日の驚異的な経済成長をもたらした生き証人であり主人公です。したがって、彼らの自伝は「この世に生きた痕跡」だけでなく、韓国の近代史と産業化過程の重要な記録になるだろう。

<g>일대기:</g>人生日記、伝記
<g>고정관념:</g>ステレオタイプ、固定観念
<g>눈부시다:</g>まぶしく、明るく
<g>현대사:</g>現代史。

explain.de:
1. Über ihr Leben nachzudenken
2. Vom Wachstum des Charakters
3. Das zeigt die Lebenseinstellung und Philosophie des Einzelnen
4. Über die moderne Geschichte und den Industrialisierungsprozess Koreas
--------------------
In letzter Zeit nehmen immer mehr Senioren zwischen 70 und 80 am Kurs „Autobiographie schreiben“ teil. Sie wollten sich von dem Stereotyp lösen, dass eine Autobiographie die Biographie einer berühmten Person sei, und wollten ihre eigene Vergangenheit dokumentieren. Die älteren Menschen, die an diesem Kurs teilgenommen und ihre Autobiografien geschrieben haben, sind lebende Zeugen und Protagonisten, die den Koreakrieg durchgemacht und das heutige phänomenale Wirtschaftswachstum geschaffen haben. Daher wird ihre Autobiographie eine wichtige Aufzeichnung der modernen Geschichte und des Industrialisierungsprozesses Koreas sein, nicht nur „die Spuren des Lebens in dieser Welt“.

<g>일대기:</g>Tagebuch des menschlichen Lebens, Biographie
<g>고정관념:</g>Stereotyp, fixe Idee
<g>눈부시다:</g>Blendend sein, hell sein
<g>현대사:</g>Die morderne Geschichte.

explain.it:
1. Di riflettere sulla propria vita
2. Della crescita del personaggio

4. Della storia moderna e del processo di industrializzazione della Corea
--------------------
Recentemente, sempre più anziani di età compresa tra i 70 e gli 80 anni seguono il corso di "Scrittura di autobiografia". Volevano liberarsi dallo stereotipo che un'autobiografia fosse la biografia di una persona famosa e volevano documentare il proprio passato. Gli anziani che hanno frequentato questo corso e scritto le loro autobiografie sono testimoni viventi e protagonisti che hanno attraversato la guerra di Corea e creato la fenomenale crescita economica di oggi. Pertanto, la loro autobiografia sarà un documento importante della storia moderna e del processo di industrializzazione della Corea, non solo "le tracce della vita in questo mondo".

explain.ru:
1. Размышления о своей жизни
2. Роста персонажа
3. Это показывает взгляд человека на жизнь и философию.
4. О современной истории Кореи и процессе индустриализации.
--------------------
В последнее время все больше и больше пожилых людей в возрасте от 70 до 80 лет проходят курс «Написание автобиографии». Они хотели освободиться от стереотипа, согласно которому автобиография — это биография известного человека, и хотели задокументировать свое собственное прошлое. Пожилые люди, которые посещали этот курс и написали свои автобиографии, являются живыми свидетелями и действующими лицами, прошедшими через Корейскую войну и создавшими сегодня феноменальный экономический рост. Поэтому их автобиография станет важным отчетом о современной истории Кореи и процессе индустриализации, а не просто «следами жизни в этом мире».

<g>일대기:</g>Дневник жизни человека, биография
<g>고정관념:</g>Стереотип, фиксированная идея
<g>눈부시다:</g>Быть ослепительным, быть ярким
<g>현대사:</g>Современная история.

explain.th:
1. การไตร่ตรองชีวิตของตน
2.เรื่องของการเติบโตของตัวละคร
3. นั่นแสดงให้เห็นมุมมองของบุคคลต่อชีวิตและปรัชญา
4. ประวัติศาสตร์สมัยใหม่และกระบวนการทางอุตสาหกรรมของเกาหลี
--------------------
เมื่อเร็ว ๆ นี้ ผู้อาวุโสที่มีอายุระหว่าง 70 ถึง 80 ปีกำลังเรียนหลักสูตร 'การเขียนอัตชีวประวัติ' มากขึ้นเรื่อยๆ พวกเขาต้องการที่จะหลุดพ้นจากทัศนคติที่ว่าอัตชีวประวัติเป็นชีวประวัติของบุคคลที่มีชื่อเสียง และต้องการบันทึกอดีตของพวกเขาเอง ผู้สูงอายุที่เข้าร่วมหลักสูตรนี้และเขียนอัตชีวประวัติของตนเป็นพยานและตัวเอกที่มีชีวิตซึ่งเคยผ่านสงครามเกาหลีและสร้างการเติบโตทางเศรษฐกิจอย่างน่าอัศจรรย์ในปัจจุบัน ดังนั้น อัตชีวประวัติของพวกเขาจะเป็นบันทึกที่สำคัญเกี่ยวกับประวัติศาสตร์สมัยใหม่และกระบวนการทางอุตสาหกรรมของเกาหลี ไม่ใช่แค่ 'ร่องรอยของการมีชีวิตอยู่ในโลกนี้'

<g>일대기:</g>ไดอารี่ของชีวิตมนุษย์ชีวประวัติ
<g>고정관념:</g>แบบเหมารวม ความคิดที่ตายตัว
<g>눈부시다:</g>ให้พร่างพรายให้สดใส
<g>현대사:</g>ประวัติศาสตร์สมัยใหม่

--------------------
Recentemente, cada vez mais idosos com idades compreendidas entre os 70 e os 80 anos estão a frequentar o curso de “Escrita de Autobiografia”. Eles queriam se libertar do estereótipo de que uma autobiografia era a biografia de uma pessoa famosa e queriam documentar seu próprio passado. Os idosos que frequentaram este curso e escreveram as suas autobiografias são testemunhas vivas e protagonistas que passaram pela Guerra da Coreia e criaram o crescimento económico fenomenal que hoje conhecemos. Portanto, a sua autobiografia será um registo importante da história moderna e do processo de industrialização da Coreia, e não apenas “os traços de vida neste mundo”.

explain.hi:
1. उनके जीवन पर चिंतन करना
2. चरित्र के विकास का
3. यह जीवन और दर्शन के प्रति व्यक्ति के दृष्टिकोण को दर्शाता है
4. कोरिया के आधुनिक इतिहास और औद्योगीकरण प्रक्रिया के बारे में
------------------------
हाल ही में, 70 से 80 वर्ष की आयु के अधिक से अधिक वरिष्ठ नागरिक 'आत्मकथा लेखन' पाठ्यक्रम ले रहे हैं। वे इस रूढ़ि से मुक्त होना चाहते थे कि आत्मकथा एक प्रसिद्ध व्यक्ति की जीवनी होती है, और वे अपने स्वयं के अतीत का दस्तावेजीकरण करना चाहते थे। जिन बुजुर्ग लोगों ने इस पाठ्यक्रम में भाग लिया और अपनी आत्मकथाएँ लिखीं, वे जीवित गवाह और नायक हैं, जो कोरियाई युद्ध से गुज़रे और आज अभूतपूर्व आर्थिक विकास किया। इसलिए, उनकी आत्मकथा कोरिया के आधुनिक इतिहास और औद्योगीकरण प्रक्रिया का एक महत्वपूर्ण रिकॉर्ड होगी, न कि केवल 'इस दुनिया में रहने के निशान'।

<g>일대기:</g>मानव जीवन की डायरी, जीवनी
<g>고정관념:</g>स्टीरियोटाइप, निश्चित विचार
<g>눈부시다:</g>चकाचौंध होना, उज्ज्वल होना
<g>현대사:</g>आधुनिक इतिहास।

<g>현대사:</g> Modern history.
explain.tr:
1. Hayatlarını yansıtmak
2. Karakterin gelişimi hakkında
3. Bireyin hayata ve felsefeye bakış açısını gösterir
4. Kore'nin modern tarihi ve sanayileşme süreci hakkında
--------------------
Son zamanlarda, 70-80 yaşları arasındaki giderek daha fazla sayıda son sınıf öğrencisi 'Otobiyografi Yazma' kursuna katılıyor. Otobiyografinin ünlü bir kişinin biyografisi olduğu şeklindeki kalıp yargıdan kurtulmak ve kendi geçmişlerini belgelemek istiyorlardı. Bu kursa katılan ve otobiyografilerini yazan yaşlılar, Kore Savaşı'ndan geçen ve bugünkü olağanüstü ekonomik büyümeyi yaratan yaşayan tanıklar ve kahramanlardır. Dolayısıyla onların otobiyografisi sadece 'bu dünyada yaşamanın izleri' değil, Kore'nin modern tarihi ve sanayileşme sürecinin de önemli bir kaydı olacak.

<g>일대기:</g>İnsan hayatının günlüğü, biyografi
<g>고정관념:</g>Stereotip, sabit fikir
<g>눈부시다:</g>Göz kamaştırıcı olmak, parlak olmak
<g>현대사:</g>Modern tarih.

#######

1. Of reflecting on their lives
2. Of the character's growth
3. That shows the individual's outlook on life and philosophy
4. Of Korea's modern history and industrialization process
--------------------
Recently, more and more seniors aged between 70 and 80 are taking the 'Autobiography Writing' course. They wanted to break free from the stereotype that an autobiography was a biography of a famous person, and wanted to document their own past. The elderly people who attended this course and wrote their autobiographies are living witnesses and protagonists who went through the Korean War and created the phenomenal economic growth today. Therefore, their autobiography will be an important record of Korea's modern history and industrialization process, not just 'the traces of living in this world'.

<g>일대기:</g> Diary of human life, biography
<g>고정관념:</g> Stereotype, fixed idea
<g>눈부시다:</g> To be dazzling, to be bright
<g>현대사:</g> Modern history.

title: [28～31]다음을 읽고 ( )에 들어갈 내용으로 가장 알맞은 것을 고르십시오. (각 2점)

content.q_text: 아무리 훌륭한 내용의 글이라도 제목이 읽는 이의 시선을 끌지 못한다면 그 글은 사람들의 관심을 얻지 못한다. 독자의 관심을 끌 수 있는 방법은 (   ) 제목을 짓는 것이다. 예를 들면 ‘돈을 관리하는 방법’보다는 ‘어느 날 당신에게 천만 원이 생긴다면? ’이라는 제목이 더 좋다. 이렇게 독자의 입장에서 제목을 붙이면 흥미를 유발하여 독자의 시선을 끌 수 있다.

explain.vi:
Dù nội dung có hay tới đâu nhưng tiêu đề không gây được sự chú ý thì bài viết cũng sẽ không nhận được sự quan tâm của mọi người. Cách để thu hút sự chú ý của độc giả là tạo một tiêu đề (   ). Ví dụ như tiêu đề 'Nếu một ngày nếu bạn có 10 triệu won?' sẽ hay hơn so với 'Cách quản lý tiền bạc'. Nếu đặt tiêu đề dựa trên lập trường của độc giả như thế này thì có thể gây hứng thú và thu hút sự chú ý của họ.
1. Đem lại sự tin tưởng cho độc giả
2. Cung cấp thông tin mới cho độc giả
3. Làm cho độc giả cảm thấy đó như là việc của mình
4. Làm cho độc giả dễ dàng đoán được nội dung
--------------------
시선을 끌다: Thu hút, lôi cuốn ánh nhìn

V + 게 하다: Khiến, làm cho.
explain.en:
1. Gives them credibility to the reader
2. Gives new information to the reader
3. Makes the reader feel like it's their job
4. Makes it easy for the reader to guess the content
--------------------
No matter how great the content is, if the title doesn't catch the reader's attention, the article won't get people's attention. One way to grab the reader's attention is to create a title that makes the reader feel like it's their job. For example, rather than ‘how to manage money’, ‘what if one day you got 10 million won? ' is a better title. In this way, if you attach a title from the reader's point of view, you can arouse interest and draw the reader's attention.

<g>시선을 끌다:</g> To catch one's eye, to attract one's eye

<g>V + 게 하다:</g> Make something do/be something.
explain.fr:
1. Leur donne de la crédibilité auprès du lecteur
2. Donne de nouvelles informations au lecteur
3. Donne au lecteur l'impression que c'est son travail
4. Permet au lecteur de deviner facilement le contenu
--------------------
Peu importe la qualité du contenu, si le titre n'attire pas l'attention du lecteur, l'article n'attirera pas l'attention des gens. Une façon d'attirer l'attention du lecteur est de créer un titre qui lui donne l'impression que c'est son travail. Par exemple, plutôt que "comment gérer l'argent", "et si un jour tu gagnais 10 millions de wons ?" ' est un meilleur titre. De cette façon, si vous attachez un titre du point de vue du lecteur, vous pouvez susciter l'intérêt et attirer l'attention du lecteur.

<g>시선을 끌다:</g>Attirer son regard, attirer son regard

<g>V + 게 하다:</g>Faire quelque chose faire/être quelque chose.

' is a better title. In this way, if you attach a title from the reader's point of view, you can arouse interest and draw the reader's attention.

1. Les da credibilidad al lector
2. Da nueva información al lector
3. Hace que el lector sienta que es su trabajo
4. Facilita que el lector adivine el contenido
--------------------
No importa cuán bueno sea el contenido, si el título no capta la atención del lector, el artículo no captará la atención de la gente. Una forma de captar la atención del lector es crear un título que le haga sentir que es su trabajo. Por ejemplo, en lugar de 'cómo administrar el dinero', '¿qué pasaría si un día obtuvieras 10 millones de wones? ' es un título mejor. De esta forma, si adjuntas un título desde el punto de vista del lector, puedes despertar el interés y llamar la atención del lector.

<g>시선을 끌다:</g>Llamar la atención, atraer la atención

One way to grab the reader's attention is to create a title that makes the reader feel like it's their job. For example, rather than ‘how to manage money’, ‘what if one day you got 10 million won? ' is a better title. In this way, if you attach a title from the reader's point of view, you can arouse interest and draw the reader's attention.

<g>시선을 끌다:</g> To catch one's eye, to attract one's eye

<g>V + 게 하다:</g> Make something do/be something.
explain.hi:
1. उन्हें पाठक को विश्वसनीयता प्रदान करता है
2. पाठक को नई जानकारी देता है
3. पाठक को ऐसा महसूस कराता है जैसे यह उनका काम है
4. पाठक के लिए सामग्री का अनुमान लगाना आसान बनाता है
------------------------
इससे कोई फर्क नहीं पड़ता कि सामग्री कितनी बढ़िया है, अगर शीर्षक पाठक का ध्यान नहीं खींचता है, तो लेख भी लोगों का ध्यान नहीं खींचेगा। पाठक का ध्यान खींचने का एक तरीका ऐसा शीर्षक बनाना है जिससे पाठक को लगे कि यह उनका काम है। उदाहरण के लिए, 'पैसे का प्रबंधन कैसे करें' के बजाय, 'क्या होगा यदि एक दिन आप 10 मिलियन जीत गए? ' बेहतर शीर्षक है. इस प्रकार, यदि आप पाठक के दृष्टिकोण से कोई शीर्षक संलग्न करते हैं, तो आप रुचि जगा सकते हैं और पाठक का ध्यान आकर्षित कर सकते हैं।

<g>시선을 끌다:</g>किसी का ध्यान आकर्षित करना, किसी का ध्यान आकर्षित करना

content.q_text: 도도새는 날지 못해 멸종된 새이다. 도도새는 천적이 없고 먹이가 풍부한 곳에 살았기 때문에 날 필요가 없었고 날려고 하지도 않았다. 그러다 도도새의 서식지에 인간과 다른 동물들이 유입되었다. 나는 법을 잊어버린 도도새는 도망가지 못해 모두 잡아먹혔고 마침내 이 세상에서 사라져 버렸다. 이처럼 우리도 (    ) 결국에는 모든 것을 잃어버리게 될 것이다.
q_answer: 내가 가진 것에 감사하지 않고 남과 비교하면

explain.vi:
Chim dodo là loài chim không bay được đã tuyệt chủng. Chim dodo không có kẻ thù tự nhiên và sống ở nơi có nguồn thức ăn phong phú nên chúng không cần bay và cũng không cần cố bay. Nhưng con người và những loài động vật khác đã tràn vào nơi cư trú của chúng. Những chú chim dodo đã quên mất cách bay không thể chạy trốn, tất cả đã bị giết thịt và cuối cùng chúng đã biến mất khỏi thế giới này. Như vậy (  ) cuối cùng chúng ta cũng sẽ đánh mất tất cả.
1. Nếu không lên kế hoạch cụ thể cho cuộc đời mình
2. Nếu không nỗ lực để ổn định với cuộc sống đã được định sẵn
3. Nếu so sánh mình với người khác mà không trân trọng những gì mình có
4. Nếu chạy trốn khỏi hiện thực vì mối quan hệ giữa người với người quá phức tạp
--------------------
유입되다: Được dẫn vào/tràn vào/du nhập
안주하다: Ổn định, hài lòng
주어지다: Được quy định, được cho sẵn
explain.en:
1. If we don't have a specific plan for our life
2. If we don't make an effort to settle into a predetermined life
3. If we compare ourselves to others without appreciating what we have
4. If we run away from reality because our relationships are difficult
--------------------
The dodo bird is an extinct bird that cannot fly. Because the dodo bird had no natural enemies and lived in a place where food was plentiful, it did not need to fly and did not try to fly. Then humans and other animals entered the Dodo's habitat. The dodo bird, who forgot how to fly, could not escape and was devoured, and finally disappeared from this world. Likewise, if we don't settle for our environment and do our best, we will eventually lose everything.

<g>유입되다:</g> To flow into, to come into
<g>안주하다:</g> To settle for, to be satisfied with
<g>주어지다:</g> To be given, to be allowed
